{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Marketing Supervisor",
  "description": "Wholesome Foods Limited is&nbsp;an Agro Processing Firm with interests in Manufacturing and Farming&nbsp;in Nigeria.&nbsp;Wholesome Foods Nigeria is a leading name agro processing sector, committed to delivering quality and safety across our supply chain.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Epe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a dynamic and multi-skilled Marketing Supervisor who possesses a valid LASRA certified commercial driving license to join our team.
	This is a unique hybrid role combining field marketing supervision with commercial driving complia
	The successful candidate will oversee marketing campaigns, manage route-to-market activities, and be certified to operate company commercial vehicles in line with LASRA safety standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate marketing activities (promotions, merchandising, brand visibility) across assigned retail routes.
	Drive commercial vehicles (when required) to execute field demonstrations and supply urgent marketing materials to outlets.
	Ensure all driving activities comply with LASRA (Lagos State Drivers&rsquo; Academy) safety and commercial driving regulations.
	Lead a team of merchandisers and brand ambassadors to achieve sales targets.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and provide weekly feedback reports.
	Maintain vehicle logs, delivery notes, and marketing inventory.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Education: OND or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, Mass Communication, or a related field.
	Experience: 2 &ndash; 4 years of proven experience in marketing, sales supervision, or field operations (FMCG/food industry preferred).
	Mandatory Certification: Valid LASRA certified commercial driving license (Class C, D, or E as applicable).
	Valid Lagos State Driver&rsquo;s License (in addition to LASRA certification).
	Ability to drive manual/automatic commercial vehicles safely.


Required Skills:


	Strong leadership and team coordination skills.
	Good knowledge of Lagos routes and retail food distribution networks.
	Basic digital reporting skills
	Excellent communication and negotiation skills.
	Defensive driving skills with a clean driving record.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary (based on experience).
	Fuel and maintenance allowance for marketing vehicles.
	Opportunity to grow into a Regional Marketing Manager role.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;happicrunch@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Marketing Supervisor&quot;&nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

Marketing Supervisor Job at Wholesome Foods Limited




        
    

        
        Wholesome Foods Limited is an Agro Processing Firm with interests in Manufacturing and Farming in Nigeria. Wholesome Foods Nigeria is a leading name agro processing sector, committed to delivering quality and safety across our supply chain.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Supervisor

Job Location: Epe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a dynamic and multi-skilled Marketing Supervisor who possesses a valid LASRA certified commercial driving license to join our team.
	This is a unique hybrid role combining field marketing supervision with commercial driving complia
	The successful candidate will oversee marketing campaigns, manage route-to-market activities, and be certified to operate company commercial vehicles in line with LASRA safety standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate marketing activities (promotions, merchandising, brand visibility) across assigned retail routes.
	Drive commercial vehicles (when required) to execute field demonstrations and supply urgent marketing materials to outlets.
	Ensure all driving activities comply with LASRA (Lagos State Drivers’ Academy) safety and commercial driving regulations.
	Lead a team of merchandisers and brand ambassadors to achieve sales targets.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and provide weekly feedback reports.
	Maintain vehicle logs, delivery notes, and marketing inventory.


Qualifications & Experience


	Education: OND or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, Mass Communication, or a related field.
	Experience: 2 – 4 years of proven experience in marketing, sales supervision, or field operations (FMCG/food industry preferred).
	Mandatory Certification: Valid LASRA certified commercial driving license (Class C, D, or E as applicable).
	Valid Lagos State Driver’s License (in addition to LASRA certification).
	Ability to drive manual/automatic commercial vehicles safely.


Required Skills:


	Strong leadership and team coordination skills.
	Good knowledge of Lagos routes and retail food distribution networks.
	Basic digital reporting skills
	Excellent communication and negotiation skills.
	Defensive driving skills with a clean driving record.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary (based on experience).
	Fuel and maintenance allowance for marketing vehicles.
	Opportunity to grow into a Regional Marketing Manager role.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Marketing Supervisor" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Lead",
  "description": "Prestigious Consulting is a professional HR consultancy firm. We focus on improving organizational performance through strategic people &amp; process management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Lead

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;/ Remote
Employment Type: Full-time /&nbsp;Senior Management

Role Overview


	We are seeking a high-performing Business Development Lead to drive revenue growth, expand market presence, and build strategic partnerships across Africa&rsquo;s manufacturing and industrial ecosystem.
	This role requires a commercially astute and strategic leader with proven experience operating across multiple sectors, particularly within manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, agro-allied, food &amp; beverages, cosmetics, and investment-driven environments.
	The ideal candidate must have experience working within or alongside a multi-sector consulting or investment firm, with the ability to navigate complex industries, structure deals, and position the company as a trusted partner in business transformation and growth.


Key Responsibilities
Strategy &amp; Revenue Growth:


	Develop and execute a comprehensive business development strategy aligned with company growth targets
	Build and manage a strong pipeline across multiple sectors and industries
	Drive revenue by identifying, qualifying, and closing high-value mandates across manufacturing and investment landscapes.


Market Intelligence:


	Conduct cross-sector market research across manufacturing, agro, FMCG, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics industries
	Monitor industry trends, regulatory changes, and competitor activity
	Translate insights into actionable, sector-specific growth strategies.


Client &amp; Stakeholder Management:


	Build and manage relationships with HNIs, DFIs, government agencies, and institutional partners
	Lead client engagements from outreach to deal closure and long-term relationship management
	Represent the company across diverse industries at events, forums, and stakeholder meetings.


Proposals &amp; Deal Execution:


	Lead development of proposals, pitch decks, and commercial offers across multi-sector opportunities
	Structure and negotiate complex deals, partnerships, and investment mandates
	Collaborate with internal teams to align BD efforts with execution capacity across different sectors.


Partnerships &amp; Team Leadership:


	Develop strategic alliances across industries including manufacturing, agriculture, cosmetics, and investment ecosystems
	Lead and manage the BD team (Partnership Lead, Social Media Manager, Research Interns)
	Build a high-performance, execution-driven culture.


Reporting &amp; Performance:


	Track KPIs (revenue, pipeline, win rate) and report directly to the Managing Partner
	Provide weekly updates and monthly performance reports.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field (MBA is an advantage)
	8+ years of experience in Business Development, Commercial Strategy, or related roles
	Proven track record of closing high-value deals across multiple sectors
	Strong experience in a multi-sector environment, including:
	
		Manufacturing
		Pharmaceuticals
		Agro-allied / Agriculture
		Food &amp; Beverages
		Cosmetics
		Investment / Advisory
	
	
	Experience working in or with a consulting firm serving multiple industries is highly preferred
	Strong understanding of African market dynamics
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.


What We&rsquo;re Looking For:


	Strategic thinker with strong commercial acumen
	Ability to operate across diverse industries and complex business environments
	Results-driven with the ability to meet aggressive targets
	Strong leadership and team management capability
	High level of professionalism, integrity, and accountability.


Salary
N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@prestigiousconsultinggroup.ng&nbsp;using &quot;Business Development Lead&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Business Development Lead Job at Prestigious Consulting




        
    

        
        Prestigious Consulting is a professional HR consultancy firm. We focus on improving organizational performance through strategic people & process management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Lead

Job Location: Lagos / Remote
Employment Type: Full-time / Senior Management

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a high-performing Business Development Lead to drive revenue growth, expand market presence, and build strategic partnerships across Africa’s manufacturing and industrial ecosystem.
	This role requires a commercially astute and strategic leader with proven experience operating across multiple sectors, particularly within manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, agro-allied, food & beverages, cosmetics, and investment-driven environments.
	The ideal candidate must have experience working within or alongside a multi-sector consulting or investment firm, with the ability to navigate complex industries, structure deals, and position the company as a trusted partner in business transformation and growth.


Key Responsibilities
Strategy & Revenue Growth:


	Develop and execute a comprehensive business development strategy aligned with company growth targets
	Build and manage a strong pipeline across multiple sectors and industries
	Drive revenue by identifying, qualifying, and closing high-value mandates across manufacturing and investment landscapes.


Market Intelligence:


	Conduct cross-sector market research across manufacturing, agro, FMCG, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics industries
	Monitor industry trends, regulatory changes, and competitor activity
	Translate insights into actionable, sector-specific growth strategies.


Client & Stakeholder Management:


	Build and manage relationships with HNIs, DFIs, government agencies, and institutional partners
	Lead client engagements from outreach to deal closure and long-term relationship management
	Represent the company across diverse industries at events, forums, and stakeholder meetings.


Proposals & Deal Execution:


	Lead development of proposals, pitch decks, and commercial offers across multi-sector opportunities
	Structure and negotiate complex deals, partnerships, and investment mandates
	Collaborate with internal teams to align BD efforts with execution capacity across different sectors.


Partnerships & Team Leadership:


	Develop strategic alliances across industries including manufacturing, agriculture, cosmetics, and investment ecosystems
	Lead and manage the BD team (Partnership Lead, Social Media Manager, Research Interns)
	Build a high-performance, execution-driven culture.


Reporting & Performance:


	Track KPIs (revenue, pipeline, win rate) and report directly to the Managing Partner
	Provide weekly updates and monthly performance reports.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field (MBA is an advantage)
	8+ years of experience in Business Development, Commercial Strategy, or related roles
	Proven track record of closing high-value deals across multiple sectors
	Strong experience in a multi-sector environment, including:
	
		Manufacturing
		Pharmaceuticals
		Agro-allied / Agriculture
		Food & Beverages
		Cosmetics
		Investment / Advisory
	
	
	Experience working in or with a consulting firm serving multiple industries is highly preferred
	Strong understanding of African market dynamics
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.


What We’re Looking For:


	Strategic thinker with strong commercial acumen
	Ability to operate across diverse industries and complex business environments
	Results-driven with the ability to meet aggressive targets
	Strong leadership and team management capability
	High level of professionalism, integrity, and accountability.


Salary
N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Business Development Lead" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Kimberly Ryan Limited&nbsp;is a leading HR consulting firm in Africa with a global footprint across Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Sierra Leone, Uganda, and the United Kingdom. We offer comprehensive human resources and advisory services that help businesses scale, streamline operations, and maximize workforce performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Teller /&nbsp;Customer Care Officer (CCO)

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Teller/CCO to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for handling customer transactions, delivering excellent customer service, and ensuring accuracy in all cash-related activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive and process cash deposits, withdrawals, and transfers
	Verify customer information and transaction details
	Maintain accurate records of daily transactions
	Address customer inquiries and provide information on banking products and services
	Ensure compliance with banking policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	HND (Upper Credit &amp; Distinction only)
	Must have completed NYSC
	Previous banking or customer service experience is an added advantage
	Strong numerical and analytical skills
	Good communication and customer service skills
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.


Salary
N200,000 / month + net, 13th Month, Leave Allowance.

Note:&nbsp;Only Shortlisted Candidates will be contacted.",

Bank Teller / Customer Care Officer (CCO) Job at Kimberly Ryan Limited




        
    

        
        Kimberly Ryan Limited is a leading HR consulting firm in Africa with a global footprint across Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Sierra Leone, Uganda, and the United Kingdom. We offer comprehensive human resources and advisory services that help businesses scale, streamline operations, and maximize workforce performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Teller / Customer Care Officer (CCO)

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Teller/CCO to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for handling customer transactions, delivering excellent customer service, and ensuring accuracy in all cash-related activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive and process cash deposits, withdrawals, and transfers
	Verify customer information and transaction details
	Maintain accurate records of daily transactions
	Address customer inquiries and provide information on banking products and services
	Ensure compliance with banking policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	HND (Upper Credit & Distinction only)
	Must have completed NYSC
	Previous banking or customer service experience is an added advantage
	Strong numerical and analytical skills
	Good communication and customer service skills
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.


Salary
N200,000 / month + net, 13th Month, Leave Allowance.

Note: Only Shortlisted Candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Human Resource Manager Job at Kiyix Recruitment Hub
content:
At Kiyix recruitment hub, we help make recruitment a stress-free experience for both the employer and the potential job seekers Our goal at KIYIX Recruitment and Motivation Hub is to help create one million jobs in Nigeria by 2030. We strive to help make businesses in Africa great by providing them with good staff and enabling the staff to become great.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Manager

Job Location: Mushin, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry:FMCG – Food Distribution

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly experienced and strategic Human Resource Manager to join our growing team in the food distribution sector.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing all HR functions, driving organizational development, and ensuring alignment with business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with company goals
	Oversee recruitment, onboarding, and talent management processes
	Manage employee relations and resolve workplace issues effectively
	Design and implement performance management systems
	Handle compensation, benefits, and payroll structure oversight
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies
	Drive training, learning, and development initiatives
	Lead succession planning and workforce planning strategies


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resource Management or related field
	5–8 years of HR experience, preferably in FMCG or food distribution
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices
	Proven leadership and people management skills
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Professional certification (e.g., CIPM, SHRM) is an added advantage.


Salary
N350,000 Monthly (Net).

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “HR Manager" as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, WhatsApp 09063916949.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mechanical Engineer",
  "description": "Vitafoam Nigeria Plc is one of the most recognized manufacturing brands in Nigeria and West Africa, primarily known for its sleep solutions and polyurethane products.

Since you are looking into engineering roles there, here is a quick overview of the company to give you some context on what they do and how a Mechanical Engineer fits into their operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Engineer
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Mechanical Engineer is responsible for ensuring the seamless operation, reliability, and maintenance of all mechanical equipment and production machinery within the manufacturing plant.
	This role involves planning and executing preventive maintenance, troubleshooting unexpected mechanical failures, and optimizing machine performance to minimize downtime and support production targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Plant Maintenance: Oversee the installation, maintenance, and repair of all mechanical systems, including industrial foam cutting machines, chemical mixers, conveyors, and pneumatic/hydraulic systems.
	Preventative Strategies: Develop and implement routine preventive and predictive maintenance schedules to extend the lifespan of factory machinery and reduce sudden breakdowns.
	Troubleshooting &amp; Repairs: Respond swiftly to equipment faults on the factory floor, diagnosing root causes and executing precise, effective repairs to get production back online.
	Process Optimization: Analyze machinery performance data to recommend modifications or upgrades that improve energy efficiency, production speed, and output quality.
	Spare Parts Management: Monitor inventory levels for critical mechanical spare parts and coordinate with the procurement team to ensure essential components are always in stock.
	Safety &amp; Compliance: Enforce strict adherence to regulatory standards and internal safety protocols during all engineering interventions, ensuring all machinery operates within safe parameters.


Educational &amp; Technical Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree (B.Sc.) or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mechanical Engineering or a related discipline.
	3&ndash;5 years of hands-on experience in mechanical maintenance, preferably within an FMCG, chemical processing, or continuous manufacturing environment.
	Proficiency in reading and interpreting technical blueprints, schematics, and mechanical drawings.
	Familiarity with CAD software (e.g., AutoCAD, SolidWorks) and maintenance management systems.


Core Competencies &amp; Soft Skills:


	Problem-Solving: Strong analytical skills with the ability to quickly diagnose complex mechanical faults and implement durable solutions under pressure.
	Workplace Safety: A rigorous, proactive attitude toward safety on the job, ensuring that all engineering activities eliminate hazards for the wider production team.
	Attention to Detail: High precision in calibrating machinery, executing repairs, and documenting maintenance logs to ensure optimal factory output.
	Communication: Clear and effective communication skills to collaborate seamlessly with machine operators, electrical supervisors, and plant managers.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;career.vitafoam@vitafoam.com.ng&nbsp;using &quot;Mechanical Engineer&quot; as the subject line of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-15",

Mechanical Engineer Job at Vitafoam Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Vitafoam Nigeria Plc is one of the most recognized manufacturing brands in Nigeria and West Africa, primarily known for its sleep solutions and polyurethane products.

Since you are looking into engineering roles there, here is a quick overview of the company to give you some context on what they do and how a Mechanical Engineer fits into their operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Engineer
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Mechanical Engineer is responsible for ensuring the seamless operation, reliability, and maintenance of all mechanical equipment and production machinery within the manufacturing plant.
	This role involves planning and executing preventive maintenance, troubleshooting unexpected mechanical failures, and optimizing machine performance to minimize downtime and support production targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Plant Maintenance: Oversee the installation, maintenance, and repair of all mechanical systems, including industrial foam cutting machines, chemical mixers, conveyors, and pneumatic/hydraulic systems.
	Preventative Strategies: Develop and implement routine preventive and predictive maintenance schedules to extend the lifespan of factory machinery and reduce sudden breakdowns.
	Troubleshooting & Repairs: Respond swiftly to equipment faults on the factory floor, diagnosing root causes and executing precise, effective repairs to get production back online.
	Process Optimization: Analyze machinery performance data to recommend modifications or upgrades that improve energy efficiency, production speed, and output quality.
	Spare Parts Management: Monitor inventory levels for critical mechanical spare parts and coordinate with the procurement team to ensure essential components are always in stock.
	Safety & Compliance: Enforce strict adherence to regulatory standards and internal safety protocols during all engineering interventions, ensuring all machinery operates within safe parameters.


Educational & Technical Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree (B.Sc.) or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mechanical Engineering or a related discipline.
	3–5 years of hands-on experience in mechanical maintenance, preferably within an FMCG, chemical processing, or continuous manufacturing environment.
	Proficiency in reading and interpreting technical blueprints, schematics, and mechanical drawings.
	Familiarity with CAD software (e.g., AutoCAD, SolidWorks) and maintenance management systems.


Core Competencies & Soft Skills:


	Problem-Solving: Strong analytical skills with the ability to quickly diagnose complex mechanical faults and implement durable solutions under pressure.
	Workplace Safety: A rigorous, proactive attitude toward safety on the job, ensuring that all engineering activities eliminate hazards for the wider production team.
	Attention to Detail: High precision in calibrating machinery, executing repairs, and documenting maintenance logs to ensure optimal factory output.
	Communication: Clear and effective communication skills to collaborate seamlessly with machine operators, electrical supervisors, and plant managers.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using "Mechanical Engineer" as the subject line of the email.
